headline: Southwest Airlines Stock Is Falling. Oil Prices Compel It to Drop Guidance.
summary: Southwest Airlines  slightly missed Wall Street’s expectations for the first quarter and discontinued its full-year guidance as the air carrier, along with the entire industry, grapples with increased fuel costs.  Wall Street had expected a first-quarter profit of 47 cents a share with sales totaling $7.27 billion, according to FactSet.  The carrier said first-quarter fuel costs were $2.73 a gallon, above its prior guidance of about $2.40, which increased fuel expenses by $164 million and represented approximately a 22 cents a share headwind.

content.title: Southwest Airlines Stock Is Falling. Oil Prices Compel It to Drop Guidance.
content.summary: Southwest Airlines  slightly missed Wall Street’s expectations for the first quarter and discontinued its full-year guidance as the air carrier, along with the entire industry, grapples with increased fuel costs.  Wall Street had expected a first-quarter profit of 47 cents a share with sales totaling $7.27 billion, according to FactSet.  The carrier said first-quarter fuel costs were $2.73 a gallon, above its prior guidance of about $2.40, which increased fuel expenses by $164 million and represented approximately a 22 cents a share headwind.
